factors.summary: Order backlog is growing due to increased demand and recent acquisitions.

evidence.text: Our sales for the second quarter of 2015 were up 8.2% over the same period last year, 4.3% of which was due to recent acquisitions. Excluding the impact of recent acquisitions, we estimate sales increased 6.3% due to increased volume...

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The growing order backlog indicates strong demand and business expansion opportunities.

factors.summary: Construction activity is growing, driven by the housing market recovery.

evidence.text: Though the level of housing starts remains below the historical average, the homebuilding industry has shown improvement since 2011. For the second quarter of 2015, actual U.S. single-family housing starts were 205,300, a 12.4% increase compared to the second quarter of 2014...

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing construction activity, especially in the housing market, suggests economic recovery and growth.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining or increasing market share, particularly with large production homebuilders.

evidence.text: Our focus is on maintaining relationships and market share with these customers while balancing the competitive pressures we are facing in servicing large homebuilders with certain profitability expectations. We expect that our ability to maintain strong relationships with the largest builders will be vital to our ability to expand into new markets as well as grow our market share.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Maintaining or increasing market share, particularly with key customers, is a strong indicator of competitive strength.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is intensifying with larger homebuilders gaining market share.

evidence.text: Over the past ten years, the homebuilding industry has undergone consolidation, and the larger homebuilders have increased their market share. We expect that trend to continue as larger homebuilders have better liquidity and land positions relative to the smaller, less capitalized homebuilders.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Intensifying competition with larger homebuilders gaining market share poses a threat to smaller competitors.

factors.summary: The company is differentiating itself through its integrated solutions and value-added products.

evidence.text: We are a leading supplier and manufacturer of structural and related building products for residential new construction in the U.S. We offer an integrated solution to our customers providing manufacturing, supply and installation of a full range of structural and related building products.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Differentiation through integrated solutions and value-added products can enhance competitiveness and market position.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to an 8.9% increase in full-time equivalent employees related to recent acquisitions and increased sales volume.

evidence.text: Our salaries and benefits expense, excluding stock compensation expense, was $56.7 million, an increase of $8.5 million from the second quarter of 2014 primarily due to an 8.9% increase in full-time equivalent employees related to recent acquisitions and increased sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor costs due to increased employees may negatively impact profit margins.

factors.summary: The company is increasing capital expenditures to support sales growth and capacity expansion.

evidence.text: The increase in capital expenditures during the six months ended June 30, 2015 primarily relates to purchasing machinery, equipment and vehicles to support sales growth and to increase capacity at existing locations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased capital expenditures indicate investment in growth and capacity expansion, which is a positive sign for future performance.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is increasing due to the ProBuild acquisition, which significantly increased its indebtedness.

evidence.text: We have substantially increased indebtedness following completion of the ProBuild acquisition in comparison to our indebtedness on a recent historical basis, which will increase our interest expense and could have the effect of, among other things, reducing our flexibility to respond to changing business and economic conditions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher debt leverage increases financial risk and could limit the company's flexibility in应对经济变化.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, with positive cash flow from operating activities.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities was $17.6 million and $0.7 million for the six months ended June 30, 2015 and 2014, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is improving, indicating strong cash generation.

factors.summary: The ProBuild acquisition is creating value by expanding the company's capabilities and is accretive to growth.

evidence.text: In addition, $350.0 million in aggregate principal amount of 7.625% senior secured notes due 2021 (''2021 notes'') remain outstanding. Our estimated liquidity upon closing the ProBuild acquisition was over $500.0 million consisting of cash on hand and borrowing availability under the 2015 facility.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The ProBuild acquisition is value-accretive and expanding capabilities.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to increased indebtedness and potential risks from the ProBuild acquisition.

evidence.text: We have substantially increased indebtedness following completion of the ProBuild acquisition in comparison to our indebtedness on a recent historical basis, which will increase our interest expense and could have the effect of, among other things, reducing our flexibility to respond to changing business and economic conditions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management caution due to increased indebtedness and acquisition risks.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include increased indebtedness and potential challenges from the ProBuild acquisition.

evidence.text: We have substantially increased indebtedness following completion of the ProBuild acquisition in comparison to our indebtedness on a recent historical basis, which will increase our interest expense and could have the effect of, among other things, reducing our flexibility to respond to changing business and economic conditions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased indebtedness and potential acquisition challenges pose risks.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to commodity price changes, which impact input costs and sales.

evidence.text: The decrease in sales for lumber and lumber sheet goods is largely due to a 9.9% decrease in market prices for lumber and lumber sheet goods in the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014, which was mostly offset by an increase in sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivity to commodity prices could negatively impact margins.

factors.summary: The economic environment is favorable, with the housing market showing signs of strength, which is a tailwind for the company.

evidence.text: According to the U.S. Census Bureau, actual U.S. single-family housing starts increased 12.4% in the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014. In the South Region, actual single-family housing starts increased 17.1% compared to the second quarter of 2014, and single-family units under construction increased 6.8% over this same time period.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong housing market provides a favorable tailwind.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products and segments, which has positively impacted the company's gross margins. This shift is driven by improved customer pricing relative to costs and an increased focus on value-added sales.

evidence.text: Our gross margin percentage increased by 2.0% during the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014, primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales.

Our gross margin percentage increased primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales in the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin products improves gross margins.

factors.summary: The company faces geopolitical risks primarily related to U.S. economic sanctions on Iran, as indicated by the presence of an Iranian national holding a mortgage with Santander UK under these sanctions. However, the Introduction section does not explicitly mention geopolitical risks, indicating a potential oversight or lack of emphasis on this factor.

evidence.text: The company is exposed to geopolitical risks related to US economic sanctions on Iran.

An Iranian national, resident in the U.K., who is currently designated by the U.S. under the Iranian Financial Sanctions Regulations and the Weapons of Mass Destruction Proliferators Sanctions Regulations ('NPWMD sanctions program'), holds a mortgage with Santander UK that was issued prior to any such designation. No further drawdown has been made (or would be allowed) under this mortgage although Santander UK continues to receive repayment installments.
No specific information on geopolitical risks is provided in the text.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Exposure to geopolitical risks related to U.S. sanctions on Iran.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power, as evidenced by consistent improvements in gross margins and successful price adjustments relative to costs. These improvements were supported by a higher mix of value-added sales.

evidence.text: Our gross margin percentage increased by 2.0% during the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014, primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales.

Gross margin increased $16.8 million to $110.6 million. In addition, our gross margin percentage increased to 24.0% in the second quarter of 2015 from 22.0% in the second quarter of 2014, a 2.0% increase. Our gross margin percentage increased primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales in the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has shown consistent improvement in gross margins and strong pricing relative to costs.

factors.summary: Overall demand is improving, with accelerating growth in single-family housing starts, particularly in the South Region, indicating a recovering homebuilding industry.

evidence.text: For the second quarter of 2015, actual U.S. single-family housing starts were 205,300, a 12.4% increase compared to the second quarter of 2014. Actual single-family housing starts in the South Region were 106,900 in the second quarter of 2015, a 17.1% increase from the second quarter of 2014.

According to the U.S. Census Bureau, actual U.S. single-family housing starts increased 12.4% in the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014. In the South Region, actual single-family housing starts increased 17.1% compared to the second quarter of 2014, and single-family units under construction increased 6.8% over this same time period.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is improving with accelerating growth in housing starts, especially in the South Region.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is mixed, showing a stable to decelerating trend. While there is a stable contribution from organic growth, it is partially offset by challenges such as commodity price deflation, which is causing a deceleration in growth. Additionally, acquisitions have contributed to overall revenue growth, but the organic component alone shows a slower increase compared to previous periods.

evidence.text: "Our sales for the second quarter of 2015 were up 8.2% over the same period last year, 4.3% of which was due to recent acquisitions. Excluding the impact of recent acquisitions, we estimate sales increased 6.3% due to increased volume..."

"Excluding the impact of recent acquisitions, we estimate sales increased 6.3% due to increased volume, which was partially offset by a 2.4% decrease due to the impact of commodity price deflation on sales."
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is mixed, with some deceleration due to commodity price deflation, but still stable overall.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are generally growing, with increases in the homebuilding market and across most product categories. However, lumber and lumber sheet goods experienced a decrease in sales due to market price deflation, despite an increase in sales volume.

evidence.text: For the second quarter of 2015, actual U.S. single-family housing starts were 205,300, a 12.4% increase compared to the second quarter of 2014. Actual single-family housing starts in the South Region... were 106,900 in the second quarter of 2015, a 17.1% increase from the second quarter of 2014.

Increased sales were achieved across all product categories, except lumber and lumber sheet goods, primarily due to increased volume. The decrease in sales for lumber and lumber sheet goods is largely due to a 9.9% decrease in market prices for lumber and lumber sheet goods in the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014, which was mostly offset by an increase in sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across most product categories, despite challenges in specific areas.

factors.summary: The homebuilding end market is experiencing positive momentum, with the single-family housing market growing fastest, particularly in the South Region, which aligns with the company's geographic focus. Housing starts have shown improvement, though they remain below historical averages.

evidence.text: Though the level of housing starts remains below the historical average, the homebuilding industry has shown improvement since 2011. For the second quarter of 2015, actual U.S. single-family housing starts were 205,300, a 12.4% increase compared to the second quarter of 2014...

According to the U.S. Census Bureau, actual U.S. single-family housing starts increased 12.4% in the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014. In the South Region, actual single-family housing starts increased 17.1% compared to the second quarter of 2014, and single-family units under construction increased 6.8% over this same time period.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The homebuilding market is showing positive momentum, particularly in the South Region, though still below historical averages.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed trends in input costs, with declining commodity prices for lumber and lumber sheet goods partially offsetting increased sales volume, while overall input costs remain stable. However, the company faces challenges in passing on price changes to customers, which may impact operating income due to short-term cost fluctuations.

evidence.text: Prices of wood products, which are subject to cyclical market fluctuations, may adversely impact operating income when prices rapidly rise or fall within a relatively short period of time.

We purchase certain materials, including lumber products, which are then sold to customers as well as used as direct production inputs for our manufactured and prefabricated products. Short-term changes in the cost of these materials, some of which are subject to significant fluctuations, are sometimes passed on to our customers, but our pricing quotation periods may limit our ability to pass on such price changes.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

evidence.text: The decrease in sales for lumber and lumber sheet goods is largely due to a 9.9% decrease in market prices for lumber and lumber sheet goods in the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014, which was mostly offset by an increase in sales volume.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining commodity prices and challenges in passing price changes to customers pose risks to operating income.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing positive margin expansion, driven by improved pricing and a higher proportion of value-added sales. Both the CURRENT OPERATING CONDITIONS AND OUTLOOK and the THREE MONTHS ENDED JUNE 30, 2015 COMPARED WITH THE THREE MONTHS ENDED JUNE 30, 2014 subsections highlight a 2.0% increase in gross margin percentage, with the latter also noting a $16.8 million increase in gross margin.

evidence.text: Gross margin percentage increased by 2.0% during the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014, primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales.

Gross margin increased $16.8 million to $110.6 million. In addition, our gross margin percentage increased to 24.0% in the second quarter of 2015 from 22.0% in the second quarter of 2014, a 2.0% increase. Our gross margin percentage increased primarily due to improved customer pricing relative to our costs and a higher mix of value-added sales in the second quarter of 2015 compared to the second quarter of 2014.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended June 30 , 2015 Compared with the Three Months Ended June 30 , 2014

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is expanding margins due to improved pricing and higher value-added sales, showing strong performance.
